name: Vojvodina (Serbia)

colors.symbolism: the historic Serbian and pan-Slavic colours
the historic Serbian and pan-Slavic colours
the historic Serbian and pan-Slavic colours
the three gold stars marking Vojvodina's traditional regions

description: Three unequal horizontal stripes — a narrow red stripe at the top, a wide central blue field, and a narrow white stripe at the bottom — with three yellow five-pointed stars arranged in a row across the blue band.
history: Adopted on 27 February 2004 by the Assembly of the Autonomous Province of Vojvodina, drawing on the historic colours of the 1848 Serbian Vojvodina (the short-lived autonomous Serb crown land within the Habsburg empire) and the pan-Slavic tricolour. The three yellow stars represent Vojvodina's three traditional regions — Banat, Bačka, and Srem — which together form the multi-ethnic northern province of Serbia. A traditional flag (without stars, reversed colour order) had been used unofficially since the 19th century; the present design was made the official provincial flag by the 2009 Statute of Vojvodina.

features.symbolism: the historic pan-Slavic colours, recalling the 1848 Serbian Vojvodina movement and the broader Yugoslav heritage of the multi-ethnic province

features.role: Three yellow five-pointed stars on the blue band
features.symbolism: the three historic regions of Vojvodina: Banat, Bačka, and Srem (Syrmia)
